title: gronophyllum procerum
intro: Gronophyllum procerum, also known as the Malabar ironwood or Malabar kadam, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. It is a large evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Gronophyllum procerum is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions.
